creator: pdemon@gmail.com
text:
Hi Yuri,

No reason in particular. It was set in the Makefile by the original author and I simply moved it into the port makefile to preserve the compilation option. I believe the original compilation target was the gcc, however, stack-protector-strong is also supported by clang.

I believe it was added in an effort to provide additional hardening as this library will be used by the core OpenVPN process which is typically external facing.

Any reason not to include it?

Phil
